text: The flammable limits of an atmosphere are the:

options.text: Maximum temperatures at which an oil source produces vapors
Upper and lower percentage of vapor concentrations in an atmosphere which will burn if an ignition source is present
Safe ambient operating thresholds for storage facilities
Minimum moisture concentrations needed to halt spontaneous ignition

explanation: Flammable limits define the range of concentrations of a flammable gas or vapor in air that will burn or explode when an ignition source is present. The lower flammable limit (LFL) is the minimum concentration, and the upper flammable limit (UFL) is the maximum concentration.
